nodes.label: Decision R1: PrismaService NestJS Integration Pattern
nodes.rationale: PrismaService extends PrismaClient with OnModuleInit ($connect), exported from a global PrismaModule via constructor DI — the official pattern, exactly replacing DatabaseService. nestjs-prisma package rejected as unnecessary wrapper; per-service instantiation rejected (no pooling/lifecycle).

nodes.label: Decision R2: Prisma with Neon Serverless
nodes.rationale: Standard postgresql provider through Neon's pooler endpoint; @prisma/adapter-neon only needed for edge runtimes, not this Node.js TCP backend.

nodes.label: Decision R3: Introspect Existing DB (prisma db pull)
nodes.rationale: Introspection guarantees the Prisma schema exactly matches production, avoiding drift; then rename to PascalCase/camelCase with @@map/@map preserving actual table/column names. Writing schema from scratch rejected due to risk of subtle differences.

nodes.label: Decision R4: Preserving PostgreSQL-Specific Features
nodes.rationale: GIN index via @@index([tags], type: Gin); CHECK constraints (ease_factor >= 1.3, interval 0-365) not expressible in Prisma schema — remain at DB level; native upsert() replaces raw ON CONFLICT; Prisma enums for card type and phase.

nodes.label: Decision R5: Interactive Transactions for Review Submission
nodes.rationale: prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => ...) gives the same ACID guarantees as the previous BEGIN/COMMIT pattern for the read-state -> compute-SM-2 -> update -> log -> increment-counter flow; batch transactions rejected (no reads inside), app-level locks rejected as unnecessary.

nodes.label: Decision R6: Explicit Soft-Delete Filtering
nodes.rationale: Explicit where: { deletedAt: null } in every query touching decks/cards — Prisma middleware and prisma-extension-soft-delete rejected as hidden complexity/over-engineering for a small codebase.

nodes.label: Decision R7: Zero-Downtime Migration Baseline
nodes.rationale: prisma db pull then prisma migrate resolve --applied 'init' marks current DB state as baseline without running SQL against the existing database; future changes go through prisma migrate dev; old custom runner and .sql files deleted.
